title: The plus sign has two jobs
why.untouched: script.js is exactly as it was.
hint2.untouched: Look at the second number in the console. It is five characters long.
why.not-closed: A tag in index.html is open and never closed.
why.not-linked: The script never reaches the page.
why.crashed: Your code stopped with an error.
hint2.crashed: Look at the Console panel below.
why.erased-source: You removed the quotes from the data.
hint2.erased-source: The numbers arrive as text and that does not change. The task is to convert them, not to rewrite where they come from.
hint3.erased-source: Every form field returns text. You type 42, you get "42". You cannot fix that at the source — you fix it where you use it.
why.total-is-text: The total is still not a real number.
hint2.total-is-text: The multiplication already works, but the result has to be a number, not text that looks like one.
hint3.total-is-text: Number("42") returns 42 — a real number. Number takes text and pulls the number out of it.
hint4.total-is-text: Wrap both names in Number before multiplying them.
why.still-glued: The sum still joins instead of adding.
hint2.still-glued: 1247 and 3 added together make 1250. If you get 12473, the plus sign has joined two pieces of text.
hint3.still-glued: The plus sign looks at what you give it. Two numbers — it adds. Text on either side — it joins. The other signs do not have this double life.
hint4.still-glued: Number on both sides of the plus.
why.wrong-count: There are not two lines in the console.
why.lost-p: A line has disappeared from the page.
blocks.0.text: Look at script.js. This time the numbers are in quotes — the way they arrive from any form field. Type 42 into a field and you get the text "42".
blocks.1.text: The multiplication gives 3741. Correct. The addition gives 12473.
blocks.2.text: The number is not wrong, the plus is
blocks.3.text: The plus sign does two different jobs. Between two numbers it adds. The moment there is text on one side, it joins.
blocks.4.text: "1247" + "3" is not a sum. Those are two pieces of text stuck together. The result is five characters, not a number.
blocks.5.text: The other signs do not have this problem
blocks.6.text: Multiplication, subtraction and division have no second job. They cannot join anything, so they turn the text into a number and calculate.
blocks.8.text: 20, then 102, then 8. The same text three times, three different attitudes towards it.
blocks.9.text: That is why the multiplication above works and the addition does not. It is not an accident and not a bug. The plus sign is busy.
blocks.11.text: Do not rely on the language guessing. Say explicitly what you want.
blocks.13.text: Number takes text and pulls out the number. Give it something that is not a number and it returns NaN — but that has a whole lesson of its own in the next section.
blocks.14.text: Why this is the most common mistake
blocks.15.text: Everything that comes from outside is text. Forms, addresses, files, answers from a server. The numbers in them look like numbers and behave like numbers in every operation but one.
blocks.16.text: So your code will work until somebody tries to add. Then the sum becomes a join, and nothing breaks.
blocks.18.text: Make both sums correct: the total should be 3741, and the two numbers added together should be 1250.
blocks.19.text: Do not touch the quotes on the first two lines. The data arrives that way, and the task is to work with it, not rewrite it.
